summary: Full backend and frontend testing of ClawMe Phase 1 landing page. All 13 backend API tests passed. All frontend flows verified via Playwright including form submission, validation, availability checks, and mobile layout.

issues: Hero headline text appears very faint/dark on initial mobile load, possibly due to animation fade-in — text becomes visible after page settles

critical_code_review_comments: Rate limiting uses in-memory store — will not persist across restarts or work correctly in multi-instance deployments (acceptable for Phase 1)
server.py is ~177 lines — acceptable for now but will need splitting when Supabase integration is added
In-memory mock (_mock_emails, _mock_handles) resets on every server restart — expected for Phase 1 mock mode
seed_data_creation: Test emails created: TEST_user_unique123@example.com, TEST_dup_email_abc@example.com, TEST_first_handle/TEST_second_handle@example.com, TEST_reserved@example.com, TEST_nohandle_xyz99@example.com, TEST_checkafter@example.com, test_ui_2026@example.com (via UI test)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All features working. Backend uses MOCKED in-memory store (Supabase not configured). Rate limiting tested implicitly. Handle availability check properly returns false after registration. Mobile layout responsive. Success state shows correctly after form submission.
